items.title: Reports: Mavericks to acquire Santi Aldama from Grizzlies
items.author: NBA.com
items.body_excerpt: Reports: Mavericks to acquire Santi Aldama from Grizzlies NBA.com

items.title: Memphis Grizzlies after trading Santi Aldama for AJ Johnson and picks.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Memphis Grizzlies after trading Santi Aldama for AJ Johnson and picks.

They will create a $17 million trade exception by absorbing AJ Johnson into their small Ja Morant trade exception.

They have that and the $29 million trade exception for taking on more money.

items.title: Mavericks reportedly land Santi Aldama in deal with Grizzlies for AJ Johnson
items.author: Yahoo Sports
items.body_excerpt: Mavericks reportedly land Santi Aldama in deal with Grizzlies for AJ Johnson Yahoo Sports

items.title: Idk man. Teams are clearly valuing picks very differently right now, but seeing how little Jaylen Brown and Santi Aldama went for (relative, of course)…and the Suns traded their ‘33 first for THAT dude…yeesh.
items.author: Gerald Bourguet
items.body_excerpt: Idk man. Teams are clearly valuing picks very differently right now, but seeing how little Jaylen Brown and Santi Aldama went for (relative, of course)…and the Suns traded their ‘33 first for THAT dude…yeesh.

items.title: The Dallas Mavericks will absorb Santi Aldama into their $20.8 million Anthony Davis trade exception.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Dallas Mavericks will absorb Santi Aldama into their $20.8 million Anthony Davis trade exception.

They will also send out AJ Johnson and the 2030 Warriors first, both of which they also got for Davis.

They essentially flipped Davis for Aldama.

items.title: The Memphis Grizzlies are trading Santi Aldama to the Dallas Mavericks for AJ Johnson, a protected 2030 Warriors first-round pick and two future second-round picks, sources tell ESPN.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: The Memphis Grizzlies are trading Santi Aldama to the Dallas Mavericks for AJ Johnson, a protected 2030 Warriors first-round pick and two future second-round picks, sources tell ESPN.
